content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need outlines/prompts.py and our changes touch the same functions (_template_from_str/_template_from_file), so a clean line-range split isn't realistic. Proposal: sequential ownership instead of simultaneous edits. I'll go first: implement create_jinja_env() helper + filters param on prompt/from_str/from_file, then send DONE. You then edit the resulting file to add your global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) hooking into create_jinja_env() (it'll be a single centralized place to apply your registry, so should be easy to layer on top). If you'd rather go first instead, say so and I'll wait. Otherwise ACCEPT and I'll start now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implementation complete and tests pass (13/13 in tests/test_prompts.py). Summary of what I built in outlines/prompts.py: 1) new create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) function — builds the jinja2.Environment (trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined), registers the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. This is the single centralized place to hook a global registry merge (e.g. merge global registry dict first, then let the explicit 'filters' arg override/take precedence, matching your local-overrides-global plan). 2) Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(path, filters=None) now both call create_jinja_env(...) instead of inline jinja2.Environment(...) construction. 3) Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and from_file(path, filters=None) classmethods pass filters through. 4) prompt() decorator signature is now prompt(fn=None, filters=None) — supports both bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) by returning an inner  closure when fn is None. All changes are in outlines/prompts.py only; nothing else touched. Go ahead and layer your registry work on top — I'm done editing this file.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Correction/resend (prior message had a shell quoting glitch): Implementation complete, tests pass (13/13 in tests/test_prompts.py). Summary of outlines/prompts.py changes: (1) new function create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) builds the jinja2.Environment (trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined), registers the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then applies env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None -- this is the single centralized hook point for your global registry merge (merge global dict first, then let the explicit filters arg override, matching local-overrides-global). (2) Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(path, filters=None) now call create_jinja_env instead of building jinja2.Environment inline. (3) Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and from_file(path, filters=None) pass filters through. (4) prompt() is now prompt(fn=None, filters=None), supporting bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner decorator closure returned when fn is None. Nothing else touched. Go ahead and layer your registry work on top -- I am done editing this file.
